channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Witkoff and Kushner to Go to Pakistan for Talks on Iran
item.description: Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff will meet Iranian officials in Pakistan. Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, will also be in Islamabad this weekend, and Iranian officials said he planned to meet with the two Americans.

item.title: US envoys will head to Pakistan on Saturday for fresh Iran talks
item.description: US envoys will visit Pakistan on Saturday for Iran talks. Live updates: US envoys will head to Pakistan on Saturday for fresh Iran talks CNN.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: 'No meeting is planned' - Iran rules out direct
item.description: Trump is sending envoys to Pakistan for talks with Iran's foreign minister. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Trump sending envoys to Pakistan for talks with Iran FM; Vance put on standby The Times of India.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire amid Hormuz uncertainty
item.description: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire amid uncertainty in the Hormuz region. Live updates: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire amid Hormuz uncertainty NBC News.

item.title: Israel kills at least 12 Palestinians in Gaza amid ‘ceasefire’
item.description: Israel killed at least 12 Palestinians in Gaza despite a ceasefire. Hamas says this escalation shows the international community failed to uphold the truce. Hamas says the Israeli escalation represents the failure of the international community to uphold the truce in Gaza.

item.title: Witkoff, Kushner to head to new Iran peace talks in Pakistan, White House says
item.description: Witkoff and Kushner will attend Iran peace talks in Pakistan. Live Updates: Witkoff, Kushner to head to new Iran peace talks in Pakistan, White House says CBS News.

item.title: Bellerin's stoppage-time strike dents Real Madrid's fading title bid
item.description: Bellerin scored a stoppage-time goal against Real Madrid. Bellerin's stoppage-time strike dents Real Madrid's fading title bid.

item.title: Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite extension of ceasefire
item.description: Israeli forces killed six Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon. This exchange of fire occurred despite an extended ceasefire. Israeli forces killed six Hezbollah fighters in an exchange of fire in the area of Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon.

item.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
item.description: The US seized vessels part of a global shadow fleet. NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U. S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.

item.title: US, Iran Prepare For 2nd Round Of Talks In Pak Without Vance, Ghalibaf
item.description: The US has confirmed that two key officials will fly to Pakistan tomorrow for talks with Iran. The talks aim to address the ongoing conflict. US Confirms Talks With Iran, 2 Key Officials To Fly To Pak Tomorrow.

item.title: Vance on 'standby' as Witkoff, Kushner to reach Pakistan on April 25 for talks with Iran, White
item.description: Iran's foreign minister is leading a delegation to Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials. The Iranian delegation will discuss regional developments and the war situation. Iranian delegation led by FM Araghchi to reach Islamabad tonight; to “hold bilateral consultations on developments in the region, as well as the latest situation” in the war.

item.title: US Justice Department Allows Use Of Firing Squads For Federal Executions
item.description: The US Justice Department approved firing squads for federal executions. The death penalty is normally carried out at the state level in the United States. US Justice Department Allows Use Of Firing Squads For Federal Executions.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law, UN warns
item.description: The UN warned that Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law. Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law, UN warns Al Jazeera.

item.title: Britain and Spain Reject Reported Plans by Trump to Punish Them
item.description: Britain and Spain rejected reported US plans to punish them for not supporting the war in Iran. An internal Pentagon email, reported by the Reuters news agency, suggested Washington was reviewing options to penalize the two nations for insufficiently supporting the war in Iran.

item.title: 23, Including US Defence Staff, Injured In Bus Accident Near Pentagon
item.description: 23 people were injured in a bus accident near the Pentagon. The accident altered mass transit operations. 23, Including US Defence Staff, Injured In Bus Accident Near Pentagon.

item.title: Trump dispatches Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for new talks with Iran’s foreign minister, JD Vance
item.description: Trump sent Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for talks. They are to meet with Iran's foreign minister. Trump dispatches Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for new talks with Iran’s foreign minister, JD Vance.

item.title: Trump dispatches Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for talks with Iran’s FM Araghchi; Vance on ‘standby’
item.description: Trump sent Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for talks with Iran's FM Araghchi. U. S. delegation would likely arrive in Islamabad on April 25, once the Iranian delegation works out the framework of talks with the U. S.

item.title: Israel poses direct threat to global security: Turkish FM Fidan
item.description: Turkish FM Fidan said Israel poses a direct threat to global security. Israel poses direct threat to global security: Turkish FM Fidan TRT World.

item.title: Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia, Putin weighs G20 attendance
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy met with Saudi leaders after securing an EU loan, continuing his diplomatic push. Russian President Putin's attendance at the G20 in Miami is uncertain, with the Kremlin saying he may or may not attend.

item.title: Netanyahu Accuses Hezbollah of "Trying To Sabotage" Lebanon Peace Efforts
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu accused Hezbollah of trying to sabotage Lebanon's peace efforts. No further details were provided on the alleged sabotage. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu (File). Netanyahu Accuses Hezbollah of "Trying To Sabotage" Lebanon Peace Efforts.
